title: Writing - Part 2
description: Choose one of the following writing tasks and write your answer in 140-190 words.

tasks.title: Letter
tasks.prompt:
You have seen an advertisement for a summer language school in the UK. Write a letter to the school asking for more information about the courses available, accommodation options, and activities provided for students outside class hours.

Write your letter.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Dear Sir or Madam,

I am writing to enquire about the summer language courses advertised on your website. I am a 19-year-old student from Spain with a B2 level of English, and I am very interested in attending a course this summer to improve my speaking and writing skills.

Could you please provide me with further details about the courses available? In particular, I would like to know whether you offer any courses specifically aimed at students who wish to prepare for the Cambridge First exam. Additionally, I would appreciate information about the class sizes and the qualifications of your teaching staff.

Regarding accommodation, I would prefer to stay with a host family in order to maximise my exposure to English. Could you let me know what is included in the homestay package and whether meals are provided?

Finally, I would be grateful if you could tell me what social activities and excursions are organised for students in the evenings and at weekends.

I look forward to your reply.

Yours faithfully,
Marta Rodríguez

tasks.title: Report
tasks.prompt:
Your college principal has asked you to write a report on the use of technology in education at your college. Include information about how students currently use technology for studying, what they find helpful, and any problems they have encountered.

Write your report.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Report on Technology Use in Education at Oakfield College

Introduction
This report is based on a survey of 150 students and aims to assess how technology is currently being used to support learning at Oakfield College.

Current Use of Technology
The vast majority of students (92%) use laptops or tablets daily for academic work. The most commonly used tools include online research databases, word processing software, and educational video platforms such as YouTube and Khan Academy. Many students also use AI tools to help check their writing.

Benefits Reported
Students highlighted several advantages, including instant access to information, the ability to organise notes digitally, and the convenience of submitting work online. Several students praised the college's virtual learning environment for making course materials easily accessible.

Problems Encountered
The main issues raised were unreliable Wi-Fi on campus, the distraction caused by social media during study sessions, and the fact that some students lack access to adequate devices at home.

Recommendations
The college should improve Wi-Fi infrastructure, run digital skills workshops, and consider a device loan scheme for students in need.

tasks.title: Review
tasks.prompt:
You see this notice on a website:

'We are collecting reviews of study apps or online learning platforms. Have you used one that helped you study more effectively? Write a review describing the platform and saying whether you would recommend it to other students.'

Write your review.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Platform Review: Anki — The Ultimate Study Tool

Anki is a free flashcard application that uses spaced repetition to help users memorise information more efficiently. Originally designed for language learning, it is now widely used by medical students, lawyers, and anyone who needs to remember large amounts of content.

The concept is simple: you create digital flashcards, and the app automatically schedules them for review at the optimal moment — just before you are likely to forget the information. This approach is backed by strong scientific evidence and genuinely works.

The interface is not the most visually appealing, and setting up your first deck can take time. However, once you invest in creating quality cards, the results are impressive. I improved my vocabulary by over 500 words in just two months of daily use.

Anki is available on desktop for free, although the iOS app costs a small fee. For dedicated students who are serious about retaining what they learn, it is absolutely worth it.

Recommendation: Highly recommended for any serious student. 4.5/5
